design_intent: Information-first shrine exploration. The player does not collect objects; they collect short contradictory records and the interface builds interpretation links.

rooms.purpose: Opening unease: an intact community shrine area with no lived-in traces.
Contradict the visible ritual name with records about sending rather than receiving.
Shift the white-fur assumption toward white clothing.
A low-complexity raised reading platform for the outfit-order record.
Name Sayo and connect the ritual rules to family memory.
Final staged reveal at the plaque, stone steps, and long-burning lamps.

clues.short_record: 迎狐 surface text, old trace like 送
white clothing for a child body
cedar base, white clothing, mugwort cover
Sayo, age nine; do not call her name; do not look back

interpretation_links.ui_result: 仪式名：迎狐 与 送狐 冲突
装束：白毛 可能是 白衣 的误读
身份：送行者 纱夜，仍记得家人

reveal_sequence: Plaque foxfire reveal: 迎狐 peels into 送狐.
Archive interpretation reveal: the records describe sending a child away.
Identity reveal: the white fox's shadow reads as Sayo, the protagonist's sister.

endings.A: Do not call her name; the ritual completes, stability returns, and back-wall cuts imply erasure continues.
endings.B: Call Sayo by name; the ritual collapses, the environment breaks, and memory begins to return.

validation_targets: All five ArchiveTrigger nodes are interactables, not pickup items.
The final plaque is gated until all clue links are available.
The stone-step platform and raised archive platform use TileMap collision, while room frames are visual props only.
Runtime backgrounds remain the existing level3 far/mid/near parallax layers.
